user_query:
I need a quick network reachability audit for a small set of hosts. Please:

1) Read input/hosts.csv (columns: group,host) and input/config.yaml (key: count for number of ICMP echo requests per host).
2) For each host, run ICMP ping using the configured count. Treat a host as reachable if it receives at least one reply; otherwise mark it unreachable. If unreachable, set avg_ms to "NA".
3) Create these files under output/:
   - output/results.csv with header exactly: group,host,sent,received,loss_percent,avg_ms,status
     • sent must equal the configured count for every host
     • received must be an integer between 0 and sent
     • loss_percent must be a number between 0 and 100 (allow decimals)
     • avg_ms must be a number for reachable hosts or "NA" for unreachable hosts
     • status must be either "reachable" or "unreachable" (lowercase)
     • Preserve the input row order
   - output/summary.json with overall and per-group aggregates in this structure:
     {
       "total_hosts": <int>,
       "reachable": <int>,
       "unreachable": <int>,
       "reachability_rate": <float between 0 and 1>,
       "groups": {
         "<group_name>": {"reachable": <int>, "unreachable": <int>},
         ...
       }
     }
     Ensure reachable + unreachable equals total_hosts, and reachability_rate == reachable / total_hosts (rounded to 6 decimals or better).
   - output/report.md containing one bullet per host in the exact format: "- <group>/<host>: <status>"; for reachable hosts append " (avg_ms: <number>)".

Use only relative paths (input/, output/). Do not write files outside output/.
